transport: One private Axios instance remains the only HTTP owner.
The invented /api/ksl base and Vite proxy were removed.
Only the exact confirmed https://wxapi.kaisaile.org origin is accepted.
Cross-origin browser access fails closed because upstream CORS is unconfirmed.
Relative paths, timeouts, JSON parsing, AbortSignal cancellation, and redacted error classification are centralized.
Generic browser Bearer headers, dynamic request bases, cookie credentials, and session metadata were removed.
repository: tournamentRepository owns the five confirmed public tournament paths.
Input schemas normalize pagination, filters, identifiers, month arrays, and optional player search.
Endpoint-specific Zod schemas validate exact response envelopes before DTO-to-domain mapping.
Recursive raw-payload discovery and speculative field aliases were removed.
Public list uid and empty pairing uid/jgid fields were removed.
Invented pairing points, start times, and synthetic replay PGN reconstruction were removed.
Finished replay is a no-request blocked repository result pending a verified guest-token lifecycle.
query: Query keys use typed normalized inputs and the web-api-v2 namespace.
Every tournament query is explicitly public and replay is explicitly private.
Only transient network, timeout, service-unavailable, and upstream failures retry once.
governance: chess-main-overseas is registered as a read-only evidence source.

residualBlocks: The password-login and URL-token absorption runtime still require removal in W2.
The confirmed upstream still grants no verified cross-origin browser CORS contract.
Finished replay still lacks an approved guest-token lifecycle.
Truthful auth, permission, unavailable, and retry UI states require W2 browser validation.
